ead.archdesc:
======= RMM06337John Nolen pamphlet collection6337Compiled by Eli BrownDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Jan. 16 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
planThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:56:50 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
John Nolen pamphlet collection
Nolen, John, 1869-1937
6337
17.4 cubic feet. 17.4 cubic feet.
Undated Pamphlets, brochures, other miscellaneous printed materials. Pamphlets relating to city planning and affiliated issues collected by John Nolen in the course of his career. Collection material in English 3375518
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Eli Brown
Date completed:
December 2003
EAD encoding:
Andrea Hektor, January 2004
Date modified:
RMC staff, June 2016
RELATED MATERIALS
A larger collection of work related to John Nolen can be found in the John Nolen papers, #2903
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
The collection has been kept in the order Nolen originally provided, cataloged by number. All un-numbered items have been placed at the end of the collection. We have attempted to provide brief, topical descriptions of the materials within a certain numbered category. Nolen also kept an alphabetical card catalog of his materials. An item can be looked up by subject, author, title, etc., and the corresponding number where the item can be found is provided. This card catalog is available for use, and is listed as box 24.
Cite As:
John Nolen pamphlet collection. #6337. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Biographical / Historical
The collection was originally given to the University of North Carolina in Chapel Hill.
Scope and content
Pamphlets relating to city planning and affiliated issues collected by John Nolen in the course of his career.
Scope and content
Also, copies of correspondence relating to the distribution of duplicate copies of the pamphlets to other institutions in 1962.
Regional planning. Housing. Highway planning Community development, Urban. City planning -- Study and teaching.

Also a publication entitled "The Abolition of Slums".46118Programs from various city planning exhibitions.55119Information on various planners, including Eliot, Griggs, Adams, and Nolen.205120-23"Royal Ordinances Concerning the Laying Out of New Towns", Spain (ordinances from the 1500s).210124Gardens and parks.240125Visions of the American city in the future.245126City planning philosophy and politics.251127Education (University lectures, courses, etc.).25421Landscape and city miscellany.26022-5Lectures and personal opinions of J. Nolen.26026City planning miscellany (includes numerous pamphlets on zoning, leisure).26527-8Creative Discussion, The Inquiry publications.26529Farm Cities Corp. of America, Virginia Farms Co., Garden Cities Ass. Of America, America Garden Cities and Town Planning Corporation.265.17210A Christmas for Cities by Gerald Stanley Lee.286211The High School as a Social Centre, by Clarence Arthur Perry.290212Suggestions for reading on city planning.294213"New" city planning ideas, planning aesthetically pleasing cities.305214Miscellaneous topics in conjunction with city planning, including architects and architecture, housing issues, transportation, and what city planning means.310215-19Effective city planning.
